response:
MOORE
MOORE'S LAW WILL LIKELY CEASE TO HOLD TRUE WHEN PHYSICAL LIMITS OF TRANSISTOR SHRINKAGE ARE REACHED, PROBABLY AROUND THE MID-2020S, AS QUANTUM TUNNING AND HEAT DISSIPATION BECOME UNMANAGEABLE AT THE ATOMIC SCALE.
***
INSTEAD OF A SUDDEN STOP, THE INDUSTRY WILL TRANSITION TO NEW ARCHITECTURES LIKE 3D STACKING, NEUROMORPHIC COMPUTING, AND QUANTUM PROCESSORS TO CONTINUE PERFORMANCE GAINS WITHOUT RELYING SOLELY ON DENSITY INCREASES.
MOORE
